p2p_enrich.notes: Added 4 pass-to-pass tests: test_repo_clipboard_ts_syntax (prettier syntax validation), test_repo_filledbutton_scss_syntax (prettier syntax validation), test_repo_clipboard_test_exists (file existence check), test_repo_constants_ts_exists (file existence check). Repo uses yarn-based CI with commands: test:typecheck (tsc), test:code (eslint), test:other (prettier), test:app (vitest). Sparse checkout prevents full yarn install, so focused on syntax-level validation via prettier which doesn't require dependencies.

validate.notes: Could not run validation: harness Bash tool is fundamentally broken in this session — every bash invocation (including `true`, `echo`, `ls`) fails with `EACCES: permission denied, mkdir '/home/worker/.claude/session-env'`. The session env directory cannot be created and even sudo/Write attempts to provision it are blocked as 'sensitive file'. Verified the same error reproduces in a sub-agent. With no bash access I cannot run `docker build`, `docker run`, pytest, or any of the four required checks. Prior validate run on 2026-04-22 (minimax) succeeded with nop=0/gold=1 and rubric ICR=1.00; the most recent pipeline failure was a rate-limit during fix_quality, not a validation regression. Task content (Dockerfile, tests/test.sh, solve.sh) was readable and looked unchanged from the last passing run, but I cannot confirm pass/fail status without exec capability. Recommend retrying in a session where the harness can write to /home/worker/.claude/session-env.

previous_validate.notes: Fixed Dockerfile missing default BASE_COMMIT value which caused the Docker build to check out the wrong commit (master HEAD instead of the buggy base commit d29fd62e). Added ARG BASE_COMMIT=d29fd62e417b8cb73d1d5dc3e207735046401491 to Dockerfile.

rubric_judge.notes: ICR=1.00 - Rubric judge passes when run against proper git repo with gold solution
